factors.name: SpaceX IPO (mega-cap tech event)
factors.summary: SpaceX completed record $75B IPO on Nasdaq Friday with shares up 19% (+25% intraday), becoming ~$2.2T market cap company. This is a major positive validation for Nasdaq as venue and could attract institutional flows into tech mega-caps; however, the move is complete and SpaceX proxy stocks are now unwinding (late-stage dynamic).

factors.name: Iran peace deal framework (risk-on macro backdrop)
factors.summary: US-Iran negotiations reportedly near framework deal with Hormuz reopening and nuclear inspections included; at least $10B in frozen Iranian funds unlocked. Risk-off pressures ease, supporting equities and reducing safe-haven demand, though deal still unsigned heading into weekend (uncertainty remains).

factors.name: Paramount-WBD merger DOJ approval
factors.summary: Justice Department cleared ~$110B Paramount-Warner Bros merger, removing antitrust overhang. Positive for media/streaming but minimal direct impact on Nasdaq 100 composition (no mega-cap tech names directly affected).

factors.name: US government AI export controls on Anthropic
factors.summary: US ordered Anthropic to halt foreign access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing national security; China also labeled additional tech firms as 'military' by US. These actions create regulatory headwind for AI sector and Nasdaq-listed AI-adjacent names; ongoing China-US tech tensions.

factors.name: Roku strategic review (sale possibility)
factors.summary: Roku exploring strategic options including potential sale; typical M&A noise with unclear timeline and impact on broader Nasdaq. Mid-cap situation, not a major NDX driver.

factors.name: Price action (4h / 24h momentum)
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.46% over 24h, -0.22% over 4h showing choppy consolidation. No decisive directional momentum; price action is flat-to-mixed into weekend with SpaceX euphoria already priced in Friday.

factors.name: Fed policy and rate expectations (forward-looking)
factors.summary: No major Fed data or FOMC decision in immediate window. BoJ rate hike (25 bps) expected Tuesday; BoE and RBA meetings also pending next week. Lack of imminent US inflation or Fed data leaves rate repricing risk muted near-term.

factors.name: 10-year Treasury yield and real yields
factors.summary: No specific Treasury yield data in news window; gold pushed above $4,200 on Iran deal optimism and inflation-hedging, suggesting real yields may be under pressure. However, no concrete yield move disclosed to confirm directional bias for duration-sensitive Nasdaq mega-caps.

factors.name: Inflation expectations and CPI calendar
factors.summary: No recent CPI, PCE, or Core CPI releases in the news window. Social Security COLA for 2027 may reach 4.7% if inflation persists, but this is forward-looking and not an immediate catalyst. Retail sales and FOMC data incoming next week.

factors.name: Semiconductor supply chain and chip export controls
factors.summary: China-US tech tensions escalating: US labeled more Chinese firms as 'military'; China 'strongly dissatisfied' with Pentagon move. Export controls on AI chips and restricted access to advanced models (Anthropic) could pressure NVDA, TSMC, semiconductor ETFs; geopolitical overhang.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 closed Friday mixed-to-positive on SpaceX IPO euphoria (record $75B raise, 19% opening-day gain) and Iran peace deal optimism, but momentum is already fading into the weekend. Positive sentiment from reduced geopolitical risk (Hormuz reopening prospect) and record venture success on the Nasdaq is offset by escalating US-China tech tensions (AI export controls on Anthropic, military designations, chip supply chain friction). The market is waiting for next week's central bank calendar (BoJ Tuesday, BoE/RBA/FOMC later) and retail sales/inflation data to reset directional bias; without fresh catalysts, price action is consolidative and choppy.
catalysts_ahead: BoE and RBA meetings (later next week)
FOMC meeting and dot plot (next week)
Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Tesla earnings (if scheduled imminent)
Iran deal signing confirmation (possibly this weekend or early next week)
China policy/tariff response to US tech restrictions
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal collapses or talks break down, geopolitical risk-off could flush equities fast. Alternatively, if Fed signals looser policy stance at FOMC, 10-year yields compress sharply lower, supporting long-duration Nasdaq mega-caps and invalidating near-term neutral bias. US-China chip wars escalation (more export bans, stricter DMA enforcement in EU) could trigger sector rotation away from mega-cap tech; monitor antitrust news and NVDA supply chain commentary closely.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in consolidation after SpaceX and Iran euphoria; avoid chasing into weekend. Wait for Monday open and reaction to BoJ Tuesday for fresh directional signal; if NAS100 breaks above 29,800 with conviction and BoJ dovish, consider long; if breaks below 29,500 on renewed China-US tech tension, short setup emerges.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Mega-Event
factors.summary: SpaceX completed largest IPO on record ($75B raise, $2.2T market cap) with shares up 19% on Friday debut. Historic milestone likely to drive broad sentiment lift and index inclusion flows into Nasdaq-100.

factors.name: Iran-US Peace Deal Progress
factors.summary: Reports indicate framework agreement near signing to reopen Strait of Hormuz, reducing geopolitical premium. Markets rallied Friday on deal optimism; crude fell below $85, reducing inflation tail-risk.

factors.name: Anthropic AI Model Shutdown (US Order)
factors.summary: US government directive forced Anthropic to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models on national security grounds. Signals tightening AI export controls and regulatory headwinds for US AI champions—potential multiple compression risk.

factors.name: China-US Tech Tensions Escalating
factors.summary: Pentagon designated major Chinese tech firms as military-linked; China rebuked US. Ongoing export controls and geopolitical friction remain a structural overhang on semiconductor and cloud capex outlooks.

factors.name: Fed/Rate Expectations & Macro Calendar
factors.summary: No major macro data or FOMC decision in the immediate window (24-72h). Central bank decisions (BoJ, BoE, RBA, Fed) loom next week but are not imminent. Real yields and nominal yields stable in context.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: No major Nasdaq-100 earnings releases or hyperscaler capex announcements in news window. SpaceX IPO success does not directly move AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA fundamentals but may lift risk-on sentiment broadly.

factors.name: Crypto Stabilization & BTC Recovery
factors.summary: Bitcoin recovered from $59K lows to trade above $63K-$64K; Standard Chartered calls crypto bottom in. Bitcoin strength historically correlates with Nasdaq-100 risk-on flows; positive backdrop for growth equities.

factors.name: Short-Term Price Action (4h/24h)
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.46% (24h), -0.22% (4h) shows muted momentum; no dramatic directional conviction yet. SpaceX IPO euphoria occurred Friday but Nasdaq-100 index itself did not surge dramatically on close.

factors.name: DOJ Paramount-WBD Merger Approval
factors.summary: DOJ green-lit $110B deal Friday, removing near-term antitrust overhang. Signals regulatory environment less hostile to M&A; may ease antitrust concerns around large-cap tech consolidation.

factors.name: Value vs. Growth Rotation Narrative
factors.summary: MarketWatch report notes value stocks outperforming growth by wide margin YTD. Potential headwind if growth multiple compression continues; Nasdaq-100 (heavy growth/tech) more vulnerable than Russell 1000 Value.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 is treading water in a mixed macro backdrop dominated by two conflicting narratives: (1) SpaceX IPO record success and potential Iran peace deal reducing geopolitical tail-risk, both BULLISH for risk sentiment and growth valuations; (2) Anthropic AI model shutdowns and escalating China-US tech/chip export controls, both BEARISH for long-duration AI/semiconductor plays that comprise >25% of NDX. Friday's modest 24h gain (+0.46%) and weak 4h action (-0.22%) suggest the index is consolidating rather than committed to a breakout. No major economic data or Fed signals have landed to reprice rates, leaving real yields stable but vulnerable.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings (if scheduled within 72h)
PCE/Core PCE inflation print (if released this cycle)
Iran deal formal signing (likely next week per Trump admin)
Nasdaq-100 inclusion of SpaceX proxy trading flows (ongoing)
screening.risk_warning: BULLISH bias invalidated if: (1) Iran deal collapses or signing delayed, reigniting geopolitical premium and crude spike; (2) CPI/PCE surprise materially hot, forcing Fed re-pricing and real yield spike; (3) mega-cap earnings disappoint or capex guidance pulls back; (4) China escalates tech restrictions or Taiwan tensions spike. BEARISH bias invalidated if: Fed signals dovish pivot, rate cuts accelerate, or AI regulatory concerns prove overblown.
screening.entry_guidance: No clear entry conviction yet. Wait for: either a 1%+ break above 29900 resistance on volume (bullish breakout), or a pullback to 29300 support on continued weakness (better risk/reward long entry). Given mixed headlines, recommend holding fire until either FOMC/macro data clarity or earnings catalyst emerges.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: SpaceX completed $75B IPO (largest ever), shares +19% on debut, valuation ~$2.2T. Massive wealth creation event and risk-on sentiment for Nasdaq-listed mega-caps; however, euphoria often precedes consolidation. Market may be pricing in this narrative already.

factors.name: Iran peace deal framework (geopolitical risk-off)
factors.summary: US-Iran interim deal framework reportedly close, with Hormuz reopening and $10-20B in frozen Iranian funds to be unlocked. Reduced geopolitical tail risk supports equity risk appetite and long-duration growth multiples (positive for NAS100).

factors.name: AI regulation & export controls tightening
factors.summary: US government disabled Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models via export control directive; China rebuked US military labeling of top tech firms. Regulatory headwinds on AI capex and foreign access could pressure mega-cap AI revenue expectations (NVDA, MSFT, META).

factors.name: Fed policy & rate expectations (unchanged near-term)
factors.summary: No major Fed surprise in news window. ECB's Nagel comments on persistent inflation suggest higher-for-longer, but no imminent US rate shift signaled. Real yields stable; growth multiple compression risk muted for now.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury yield (no fresh data)
factors.summary: No new Treasury or inflation data in 24h window. Without CPI/PCE print or FOMC, yield trajectory unclear; tech long-duration sensitivity remains latent.

factors.name: VIX / volatility sentiment
factors.summary: SpaceX euphoria and Iran deal optimism suggest risk-on tone, but NAS100 price action is modest (+0.46% 24h, -0.22% 4h). No volatility spike or squeeze signal; market digesting catalysts calmly.

factors.name: Dollar strength (USD impact on NQ international revenue)
factors.summary: DXY ~99.80 (down 0.27% on week per FXStreet). No major USD tailwind or headwind; most NQ components have healthy international diversification but near-term FX backdrop is neutral.

factors.name: Crypto correlation (risk-on proxy)
factors.summary: Bitcoin recovered to $63-64k range after $59k low earlier in week; Standard Chartered calls crypto bottom in place. Risk-on sentiment reflected in BTC strength should support NQ risk assets.

factors.name: Tech sentiment & earnings visibility
factors.summary: No mega-cap earnings or forward guidance in news window. SpaceX IPO is SpaceX-specific event, not Nasdaq index constituent yet. NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings cycle still distant; no visibility surprises.

factors.name: Antitrust & regulatory approvals
factors.summary: DOJ approved Paramount-WBD merger (~$110B deal), clearing a major deal uncertainty. No new antitrust actions on mega-caps reported; regulatory backdrop mildly constructive.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two competing narratives: SpaceX's record $75B IPO and imminent Iran peace deal both support risk-on sentiment and long-duration growth multiples (bullish for tech); however, tightening AI export controls and regulatory scrutiny on mega-cap AI capex are headwinds. Price action is muted (+0.46% 24h), suggesting market is pricing in catalysts but not yet committing to a directional breakout. Fresh geopolitical stability could unlock a relief rally, but AI regulation risks cap upside.
catalysts_ahead: Retail sales data (US, likely early next week)
FOMC meeting decision (likely mid-late June per typical calendar)
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings dates (check July calendar)
Iran deal signing (expected next few days, per Trump admin comment)
China policy response to US tech export controls
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal falls apart or geopolitical tensions re-escalate, risk-off reversal could trigger 1–2% NAS100 dip. Conversely, if AI export controls ease or mega-cap earnings beat expectations, upside to 30,200–30,500 is possible. Lack of major data release in next 24h means event risk is muted; watch for surprise inflation print or Fed guidance shift.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in early-stage risk-on phase but price action is tepid (move not yet confirmed). On weakness to 29,400–29,500, consider long entry with target 30,000 if Iran deal signed and AI regulation fears ease. Avoid chasing near resistance; await pullback or fresh catalyst confirmation.
